_comment: Manual difficulty overrides. Format: avibase-ID -> {difficultyScore: 0-100, difficultyRating: 1-10}
_section_urban_introduced: Introduced/urban species rated too hard because EBD has poor urban cell coverage

avibase-BBA263C2._reason: Ubiquitous in every city worldwide; algorithm sees few cells due to poor urban EBD coverage

avibase-35810386._reason: Noisy, conspicuous flocks around nest structures in many US cities; easy once you know the site

avibase-8B85CB2C._reason: Loud, conspicuous feral flocks in FL, CA, TX; easy at known roost sites

avibase-B42BCB40._reason: Established feral flocks in Miami area; findable at known sites

avibase-31C03CB8._reason: Established feral population in Phoenix metro; easy at neighborhood feeders there

avibase-8DE71518._reason: Small feral flocks in a few cities; localized but findable at known roost sites

avibase-12E354E2._reason: Small feral flocks in San Francisco; localized but findable at known roost sites

avibase-B1595124._reason: Established feral populations in CA and TX cities; findable at known roost/nesting sites

_section_nocturnal: Nocturnal/crepuscular species: harder than data suggests because most birding happens during daytime

avibase-FC366114._reason: Nocturnal but widespread and vocal; responds to playback; occasionally seen at dusk

avibase-BDBB04D5._reason: Nocturnal but frequently heard/seen at dusk in suitable habitat; vocal and responsive

avibase-A69062C8._reason: Strictly nocturnal; small, well-camouflaged; rarely seen without targeted effort or playback

avibase-89F8B6F5._reason: Strictly nocturnal; small, well-camouflaged; rarely seen without targeted effort or playback

avibase-6B54759B._reason: Strictly nocturnal; rarely encountered without nest box sites or targeted nighttime effort

avibase-26BA25EF._reason: Strictly nocturnal, tiny, secretive; very hard to find without banding stations or playback

avibase-FA36ABE5._reason: Genuinely rare and hard to find; requires travel to remote northern forests

avibase-FC4D40D2._reason: Strictly nocturnal, secretive, well-camouflaged; seldom found outside known winter roost sites

avibase-5B23B360._reason: Irruptive; easy in invasion years but nearly impossible in off years; unpredictable

avibase-1BF907B6._reason: Strictly nocturnal, tiny, montane forests; one of the hardest owls to find

avibase-D74BA965._reason: Strictly nocturnal, tiny; limited range in AZ/TX borderlands; requires nighttime effort

avibase-2E2B24EB._reason: Strictly nocturnal; restricted to SE AZ sky island canyons; requires nighttime effort

avibase-8AB4EAF6._reason: Strictly nocturnal, remote boreal forests; very difficult to find even with effort

avibase-24E39ACD._reason: Crepuscular but conspicuous overhead in season; visible during daytime migration

avibase-D621FFC6._reason: Strictly nocturnal; sits on roads at night but almost never found during daytime

avibase-8D3E8871._reason: Strictly nocturnal; heard far more than seen; requires nighttime effort in SE US forests

avibase-1A09655B._reason: Strictly nocturnal; heard far more than seen; requires nighttime effort in forests

avibase-CED2468C._reason: Strictly nocturnal; limited range in SW mountains; requires nighttime effort

avibase-C0F335B7._reason: Strictly nocturnal; extremely range-restricted in US (a few AZ canyons); requires nighttime effort

avibase-F2CB69EC._reason: Nocturnal but sits on roads at night in S Texas; findable with effort at known sites

_section_secretive_marsh: Secretive marsh birds: harder than frequency suggests because they hide in dense vegetation

avibase-2CC21529._reason: Very secretive; heard far more than seen; rarely flushes; requires marsh-edge patience

avibase-87705CC4._reason: Secretive marsh bird; occasionally responds to playback but rarely seen in open

avibase-13E9F9B4._reason: Extremely well-camouflaged; stands motionless in reeds; heard far more than seen

avibase-0B361ED8._reason: Tiny, secretive, well-camouflaged; one of the hardest regular marsh birds to see

avibase-F4829920._reason: Crepuscular display flights are findable, but daytime detection nearly impossible; requires timed effort

_section_pelagic: Pelagic species: require boat trips to find, which most birders don't do regularly

avibase-D295D249._reason: Requires pelagic boat trip; common at sea but inaccessible to most birders

avibase-C5FB15C6._reason: Requires pelagic boat trip; breeds on remote islands; nocturnal at colonies

avibase-DCD73C3A._reason: Requires dedicated far-offshore pelagic trip; globally endangered; very hard to find

avibase-D3FAE385._reason: Requires far-offshore pelagic trip; uncommon and hard to identify at sea

avibase-710BB4E6._reason: Requires pelagic trip off Atlantic coast; can be common on good trips but inaccessible otherwise

avibase-8B879DFA._reason: Requires pelagic trip; can be common off Atlantic coast on right trip but inaccessible otherwise

avibase-1725B07D._reason: Visible from shore on Pacific coast but requires pelagic trip on Atlantic; mixed accessibility

avibase-5AEDC002._reason: Requires pelagic trip off Atlantic coast; uncommon; occasionally from shore during storms

avibase-7D1A9D5C._reason: Requires pelagic trip; uncommon and difficult to find even at sea

avibase-7CB8F5B7._reason: Pelagic; occasionally seen from shore during migration but usually requires boat trip

avibase-39086887._reason: Pelagic; occasionally seen from shore but usually requires coastal persistence or boat trip

avibase-1D446440._reason: Pelagic in winter; breeds in remote Arctic tundra; requires effort to find in either context

avibase-05520B42._reason: Tropical; visible from some piers/jetties in FL/TX but limited access for most birders

avibase-0A4609D1._reason: Tropical pelagic; mostly seen in Hawaii or on pelagic trips; rare from mainland shore

avibase-534FB490._reason: Visible from Atlantic shore in season; requires coastal trip but not a pelagic

avibase-8AA3B42E._reason: Visible from shore in FL/TX coastal areas; widespread in tropics; not hard with coastal access

avibase-EE8ECB1D._reason: Requires Pacific pelagic trip; common on Monterey Bay trips but inaccessible without a boat

avibase-98FB6D4B._reason: Requires Pacific pelagic trip or trip to Hawaii breeding colonies; inaccessible for most

_section_range_restricted: Range-restricted species: harder or easier than algorithm suggests due to localized populations

avibase-B9A2B7CC._reason: Breeds almost exclusively in N Michigan jack pine; very range-restricted but findable on guided tours

avibase-D78D24B9._reason: Florida endemic; very range-restricted but tame and easy to find at known reserves

avibase-8A35BFD6._reason: Breeds only in central TX juniper-oak woodlands; range-restricted but findable by song in season

avibase-DEFD8414._reason: Very range-restricted in TX/OK; formerly endangered; requires specific habitat knowledge

avibase-0BFCAE79._reason: Restricted to managed longleaf pine; requires visiting specific military bases/refuges with active colonies

avibase-24F14328._reason: Extremely range-restricted but conspicuous and easy to find at known sites (Big Sur, Grand Canyon, Pinnacles)

avibase-B87D744D._reason: Critically endangered with tiny population; findable at Aransas NWR in winter but requires specific trip

_section_secretive_grassland: Secretive grassland/skulking species: harder than frequency suggests

avibase-CDA2F827._reason: Extremely skulking in dense grasslands; weak song easily missed; declining habitat specialist

avibase-727D994F._reason: Very skulking in dense marsh; runs through grass rather than flying; hard to see well

avibase-79C9C8C4._reason: Restricted to NE US salt marshes; skulking and declining; range-restricted and secretive

avibase-BF380B5D._reason: High-altitude song flight makes it detectable but seeing it on ground is very difficult; grassland specialist

_section_hawaiian: Hawaiian species: algorithm underrates many because few cells in dataset but some are genuinely findable

avibase-7B3AB998._reason: Restricted to Big Island but conspicuous soaring raptor; not hard to find there

avibase-5E3BDB15._reason: Restricted to Hawaii but conspicuous on wetlands and golf course ponds; easy on-island

avibase-EDB18078._reason: Restricted to Hawaii but conspicuous and habituated to people at parks; easy on-island

avibase-374CF449._reason: Common in native forest on Big Island and Maui; easy at Hakalau or Hosmer Grove

avibase-ABB15738._reason: Common Hawaiian honeycreeper; even found at lower elevations; easy in native forest

avibase-A71C0CB7._reason: Conspicuous red honeycreeper in native forest; declining but findable at high elevations

avibase-0E48C9D0._reason: Curious and conspicuous in native forest; findable at Hakalau NWR

avibase-E2F7F6EC._reason: Critically endangered with tiny population on Oahu; very hard to find

avibase-D94752EA._reason: Restricted to high-elevation Maui forest; findable at Polipoli or Hosmer Grove with effort

avibase-B690DF97._reason: Critically endangered; restricted to highest-elevation native forest on Big Island

avibase-DDE78CC9._reason: Critically endangered; restricted to remote high-elevation Big Island forest; very hard

avibase-DD65F272._reason: Critically endangered; restricted to remote high-elevation Big Island forest; very hard

avibase-6B31D099._reason: Critically endangered; restricted to mamane forest on Mauna Kea; accessible with 4WD road but very rare
